from.title: 1 Peter 3 Baptism Like Flood
from.body: # 1 Peter 3: Baptism Like the Flood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 3:18-22 Eight souls were saved through water in Noah's ark; baptism corresponds to this. The flood typifies the cleansing-and-deliverance of Christian baptism. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 7-8** — Noah's flood and ark. - **Gen 6:5-8** — Wickedness met by judgment. - **Ezek 14:14** — Noah, Daniel, and Job — paradigmatic righteous. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Flood Baptism
to.body: # Flood / Ark → Baptism Into Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 6-9 A righteous remnant is preserved in a wooden vessel as God's judgment washes the world clean. Noah's family passes through the waters of death and emerges into a new creation, greeted by a covenant rainbow. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 3:20-21: "Baptism, which corresponds to this, now saves you" — through-water salvation in Christ's resu…

from.title: Revelation 17 Babylon The Harlot
from.body: # Revelation 17: Babylon the Harlot ## NT Text — Rev 17 A great harlot sits on many waters, drunken with the blood of the saints. Babylon-Tyre prophecies converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 47** — Lament over Babylon. - **Jer 50-51** — Babylon's fall. - **Ezek 16; 23** — Faithless harlot Jerusalem. ## Shared structure - Harlot imagery - Sitting on waters - Cup of abominations - Adornment in gold -…

to.title: Revelation 18 Babylon Fallen
to.body: # Revelation 18: Babylon Fallen ## NT Text — Rev 18 Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. Merchants and kings lament her destruction; the saints rejoice. Jeremiah and Ezekiel laments echoed. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 21:9** — Fallen, fallen is Babylon. - **Jer 51** — Detailed Babylon-doom. - **Ezek 27** — Lament over Tyre. ## Shared structure - Fallen-fallen formula - Merchant lament - Saints rejoi…

from.title: pneuma
from.body: # pneuma (πνεῦμα) — spirit / breath / wind ## Semantic field Wind, breath, spirit — paralleling Hebrew ruach. Applied to wind (John 3:8), human spirit, evil spirits, and the Holy Spirit. ## Theological weight Pneuma Hagion — the Holy Spirit, third person of the Trinity, poured out at Pentecost. The pneumatic life is the life "in" the Spirit — eschatological power within the present age. ## Septuag…

to.title: ruach
to.body: # ruach (רוּחַ) — spirit / breath / wind ## Semantic field Moving air — wind, breath, spirit. Used of weather, human life-breath, and the Spirit of God. The same word covers the rustling wind, the gasp of a dying man, and the hovering Spirit of Genesis 1:2. ## Theological weight Creation, prophetic empowerment, and resurrection all involve ruach. Ezekiel 37 binds them: the Spirit-wind breathes lif…

from.title: Lamentations 3 New Mercies
from.body: # Lamentations 3 / New Every Morning → God's Faithfulness in Suffering ## OT Story — Lamentations 3:22-26 'The steadfast love of the LORD never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.' Hope amid ruin. ## NT Fulfillment - 2 Corinthians 1:3-4: Father of mercies, God of all comfort. - 1 Peter 5:7: Cast your anxieties on him. - Romans 8:28: All…

to.title: Lamentations Suffering City
to.body: # Lamentations / Weeping Over the City → Christ's Tears Over Jerusalem ## OT Story — Lamentations 1-3 Jeremiah laments the fallen city: 'How lonely sits the city that was full of people… Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by?' He bears the people's sorrow. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:41-44: Jesus wept over Jerusalem. - Matthew 23:37-39: Lament over Jerusalem. - Lamentations 3:22-23: His mercie…

from.title: 1 Thess 4 Coming Trumpet
from.body: # 1 Thess 4: Coming with the Trumpet ## NT Text — 1 Thess 4:13-18 The Lord descends from heaven with a cry of command, the archangel's voice, and the trumpet of God; the dead in Christ rise and the living are caught up. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:16** — Trumpet at Sinai. - **Isa 27:13** — Great trumpet, scattered gathered. - **Dan 12:1-2** — Michael arises; many awake. ## Shared structure - Tru…

to.title: Rapture Thessalonians
to.body: # Rapture (1 Thessalonians 4) ## The oracle The Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel and the trumpet of God; the dead in Christ rise first, then the living are caught up together with them to meet the Lord in the air. ## Date / context Paul to grieving Thessalonians, c. AD 51. ## Fulfillment Awaits the parousia; Paul universalizes the trumpet-…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

to.title: Levites Set Apart
to.body: # Levites Set Apart in Place of Firstborn → Royal Priesthood ## OT Story — Numbers 3:11-13; 8:14-19 God claims every firstborn of Israel for himself; he takes the Levites instead. They serve at the tabernacle on behalf of the people. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Peter 2:9: A chosen race, a royal priesthood. - Revelation 1:6: He made us a kingdom, priests to his God. - Exodus 19:6: A kingdom of priests an…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

from.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
from.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

to.title: Gall And Vinegar Offered
to.body: # Gall and Vinegar Offered ## NT Text — Matt 27:34, 48 Jesus is offered gall (refused) and later sour wine (received) — Psalm 69:21 fulfilled. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Gall and vinegar. - **Prov 31:6** — Strong drink for perishing — refused. ## Shared structure - Bitter drink - Refusal of narcotic - Fulfillment - Mock-mercy ## Theological motif The cup of substitution cannot be drunk dul…

from.title: Ark Captured And Returned
from.body: # Ark Captured and Returned → Christ Captured, Yet Triumphant ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 4-6 Philistines capture the ark. In their temple, Dagon falls before it and is shattered. Plagues afflict their cities. They send the ark back on a new cart. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 4:8: When he ascended, he led captivity captive. - Colossians 2:15: He disarmed the powers, triumphing over them. - Psalm 68:18…

to.title: Dagon Falls
to.body: # Dagon Falls Before the Ark → Idols Cannot Stand Before Christ ## OT Story — 1 Samuel 5:1-5 Philistines place the ark in Dagon's temple. Morning one: Dagon face-down before the ark. Morning two: Dagon's head and hands cut off on the threshold. ## NT Fulfillment - 1 John 5:21: Keep yourselves from idols. - Acts 19:18-20: Magic books burned where the gospel prevails. - Isaiah 46:1-7: Bel bows, Nebo…

from.title: Psalm 110 Priest King
from.body: # Psalm 110 / 'Sit at My Right Hand' → Priest-King Forever ## OT Story — Psalm 110 'The LORD says to my Lord: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool… You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 22:41-46: Jesus' messianic riddle from this psalm. - Hebrews 5:6; 7:1-28: Christ's eternal priesthood after Melchizedek. - Acts 2:34-36: Pete…

to.title: Psalm 2 Anointed King
to.body: # Psalm 2 / 'You Are My Son' → Messiah Enthroned Despite Nations' Rage ## OT Story — Psalm 2 Nations rage and kings plot against the LORD and his anointed. The LORD laughs; he installs his King on Zion. 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you. Ask of me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 4:25-28: Quoted as fulfilled in the plot against Jesus. - Acts 13:3…

from.title: Water Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

to.title: Wine Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Wine Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 2; Matt 26 Noah's vineyard, Melchizedek's wine, Passover cups, Isaiah's wine-feast, Cana's water-into-wine, the cup of the new covenant, the cup of wrath — wine threads the canon. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 9:20** — Noah's vineyard. - **Gen 14:18** — Melchizedek's bread and wine. - **Isa 25:6** — Aged wine on the mountain. - **Jer 25:15** — Cup of wrath…

from.title: Day Of The LORD Amos
from.body: # Day of the LORD (Amos) ## The oracle "Woe to you who desire the day of YHWH! Why would you have the day of YHWH? It is darkness, and not light" — as if one fled from a lion and met a bear. ## Date / context Amos to northern Israel under Jeroboam II, c. 760 BC, in luxurious complacency. ## Fulfillment The day fell upon Samaria in 722 BC at Assyrian hands and continues to be paradigmatic for divin…

to.title: Day Of The LORD Joel
to.body: # Day of the LORD (Joel) ## The oracle The day of YHWH is great and very awesome — darkness, locust-army, cosmic upheaval — yet whoever calls on the name of YHWH shall be saved, and his Spirit will be poured out on all flesh. ## Date / context Joel, dating disputed (9th-5th c. BC), prompted by a devastating locust plague read as eschatological harbinger. ## Fulfillment Peter applies Joel 2:28-32 t…

from.title: Isaiah 11 Branch Of Jesse
from.body: # Branch from Jesse's Stump → Peaceable Kingdom of the Root ## OT Story — Isaiah 11:1-10 From the stump of Jesse a shoot comes; the Spirit rests on him. He judges with righteousness. The wolf dwells with the lamb. The earth is full of the knowledge of the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 15:12: 'The root of Jesse will come; in him the Gentiles will hope.' - Revelation 5:5: The Root of David has co…

to.title: Jeremiah 23 Righteous Branch
to.body: # Righteous Branch / The LORD Our Righteousness → Christ Our Righteousness ## OT Story — Jeremiah 23:5-6 'I will raise up for David a righteous Branch, who shall reign as king. This is the name by which he shall be called: The LORD is our righteousness.' ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 3:21-22: The righteousness of God revealed through faith in Jesus Christ. - 1 Corinthians 1:30: Christ became to us ri…

from.title: Revelation 21 New Jerusalem Descending
from.body: # Revelation 21: New Jerusalem Descending ## NT Text — Rev 21:1-22:5 John sees the new heaven and new earth and the New Jerusalem descending from heaven as a bride adorned. God dwells with men. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 65:17-25** — New heavens and new earth. - **Ezek 40-48** — Vision of restored temple-city. - **Zech 8:3** — I will dwell in Zion. ## Shared structure - New cosmos - City descending…

to.title: Revelation 22 River Of Life Tree Of Life
to.body: # Revelation 22: River and Tree of Life ## NT Text — Rev 22:1-5 A river of life flows from the throne; the tree of life bears twelve fruits. Eden's lost garden and Ezekiel's temple-river converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:9-10** — Eden's tree and river. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. - **Joel 3:18** — Fountain from the house of the LORD. ## Shared structure - River from throne - Tree of…

from.title: Apocalypse Of Weeks Enoch
from.body: # Apocalypse of Weeks → Periodized History and Final Age ## OT Story — 1 Enoch 93; 91:11-17 History is divided into ten 'weeks'; the seventh sees apostasy and the chosen of the eternal plant; later weeks bring judgment and new creation. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 24:14: This gospel will be preached throughout the world, and then the end. - 2 Peter 3:13: New heavens and new earth. - Galatians 4:4:…

to.title: Daniel 9 Seventy Weeks
to.body: # Seventy Weeks of Daniel → The Time of Messiah ## OT Story — Daniel 9:24-27 Seventy weeks are decreed to finish transgression, atone for iniquity, and bring everlasting righteousness. An anointed one will come and be cut off. ## NT Fulfillment - Galatians 4:4: When the fullness of time had come, God sent his Son. - Luke 24:44: Everything written about me must be fulfilled. - Mark 1:15: The time i…

from.title: Dedication Of Temple
from.body: # Dedication of the Temple ## What it is The inaugural festival at the consecration of the temple — first under Solomon, then in the second-temple rededication under Ezra, and again under the Maccabees — when the glory of YHWH filled the sanctuary. ## Key elements - Massive sacrificial offerings - Solomon's intercessory prayer - Glory cloud filling the house - Fourteen days of feasting (1 Kgs 8) -…

to.title: Glory Filling Temple Solomon
to.body: # Glory Filling Solomon's Temple ## The appearance At the temple dedication, the cloud fills the house of YHWH so that the priests cannot stand to minister; fire descends and consumes the offerings, and the glory of YHWH fills the house. ## Mode / context Solomon's dedication, c. 957 BC; covenant-renewal liturgy. ## Significance The Sinai-tabernacle pattern reaches its architectural climax — YHWH …

to.title: Glory On Sinai
to.body: # Glory on Sinai ## The appearance The mountain is wrapped in thick cloud, thunder, lightning, and trumpet blast; YHWH descends in fire, the mountain quakes, and Moses ascends into the cloud where the glory dwells; later YHWH passes by, proclaiming his name. ## Mode / context Covenant-making at Sinai; the constitutive theophany of Israel. ## Significance The encounter that founds the covenant and …

from.title: Macedonian Call Vision
from.body: # Macedonian Call Vision ## NT Text — Acts 16:6-10 A man of Macedonia appears in a vision pleading for help. The gospel crosses into Europe by Spirit-direction. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 12:1** — Go from your country. - **Ezek 1** — Spirit guidance of the prophet. - **Isa 49:12** — From the north and from the west. ## Shared structure - Vision in the night - Geographic redirection - Spirit-prevent…

to.title: Pauls First Missionary Journey
to.body: # Paul's First Missionary Journey ## NT Text — Acts 13-14 Paul and Barnabas, sent by the Antioch church, take the gospel to Asia Minor synagogues. The Jew-first, then Gentile pattern is established. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 49:6** — Light to nations — Paul cites it. - **Joel 2:32** — Whoever calls on the name. - **Hab 1:5** — Behold and wonder — Paul preaches it. ## Shared structure - Sending by …

from.title: New Heavens New Earth Isaiah
from.body: # New Heavens and New Earth (Isaiah) ## The oracle "Behold, I create new heavens and a new earth, and the former things shall not be remembered" — Jerusalem a joy, no more weeping, infants not dying, wolf and lamb feeding together. ## Date / context Isaiah's final restoration oracles, exilic-postexilic horizon. ## Fulfillment 2 Peter 3:13 and Revelation 21:1 take up the language for the consummati…

to.title: New Jerusalem Descending
to.body: # New Jerusalem Descending ## The oracle John sees the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride; God dwells with humanity, every tear wiped away, no more death, mourning, or pain. ## Date / context Climax of Revelation; Ezekiel's temple vision is a major OT antecedent. ## Fulfillment Awaits consummation; foretasted in the church as the city set on a hill. #…

from.title: Zechariah 11 Thirty Pieces
from.body: # Thirty Pieces of Silver → Judas' Betrayal Price ## OT Story — Zechariah 11:12-13 The good shepherd asks his wages; they weigh out thirty pieces of silver. 'Throw it to the potter — that lordly price they have set on me.' He throws it into the house of the LORD to the potter. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 26:15: They paid Judas thirty pieces of silver. - Matthew 27:3-10: Judas throws it back; the c…

to.title: Zechariah 13 Strike The Shepherd
to.body: # Strike the Shepherd → Sheep Scattered at Gethsemane ## OT Story — Zechariah 13:7 'Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me… Strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 26:31: 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.' - Mark 14:27: Same citation at Gethsemane. - John 16:32: You will be scattered to your …

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
to.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

from.title: Joseph In The Pit
from.body: # Joseph in the Pit → Descent and Deliverance ## OT Story — Genesis 37:18-24 The brothers strip Joseph of his ornate robe and cast him into an empty cistern in the wilderness. From the pit he is drawn up and sold into Egypt. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 88:6: 'You have put me in the depths of the pit' — lament that anticipates Christ's descent. - Acts 2:24: Death could not hold him. - Ephesians 4:9-1…

to.title: Joseph Reveals Self
to.body: # Joseph Reveals Himself → Israel Recognizes the Pierced One ## OT Story — Genesis 45:1-15 After testing his brothers, Joseph dismisses his attendants and weeps aloud: 'I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?' The brothers are terrified, then reconciled. He sends them back to bring their father. ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 7:13: Joseph 'made himself known to his brothers' — Stephen uses the verb as ty…

from.title: Crucifixion Between Two Thieves
from.body: # Crucifixion Between Two Thieves ## NT Text — Luke 23:32-43 Jesus is crucified between two criminals, fulfilling Isaiah's 'numbered with the transgressors.' One mocks, one believes — a microcosm of human response. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:12** — Numbered with the transgressors. - **Ps 22:16** — Pierce my hands and feet. - **Zech 13:7** — Strike the shepherd. ## Shared structure - Three crosse…

to.title: Simon Of Cyrene Helper
to.body: # Simon of Cyrene Bears the Cross ## NT Text — Mark 15:21 Simon of Cyrene is compelled to carry the cross with Jesus. The reluctant helper foreshadows cruciform discipleship. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4** — He bore our griefs. - **Lev 16:21-22** — Hands laid on the goat. - **Ps 22:7** — Mocked surroundings. ## Shared structure - Stranger drawn in - Bearing wood - Path to execution - Implicit su…

from.title: Burning Bush
from.body: # Burning Bush ## The appearance The angel of YHWH appears to Moses in a flame of fire from the midst of a bush that burns yet is not consumed; God speaks from the bush, calls Moses by name, reveals the name "I AM," and commissions him. ## Mode / context Far side of the wilderness, at Horeb, the mountain of God. ## Significance Disclosure of the divine name (YHWH); commission of the deliverer; gro…

to.title: Pillar Of Cloud And Fire
to.body: # Pillar of Cloud and Fire ## The appearance YHWH goes before Israel by day in a pillar of cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire, to lead them; the same pillar stands between Israel and the Egyptians at the sea. ## Mode / context Throughout the wilderness wanderings; visible, mobile, and protective. ## Significance Continuous divine presence guiding the people; ancestor of the shekhinah glory th…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Living Hope
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Living Hope ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:3-9 Peter blesses God for a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ. The inheritance is imperishable, undefiled, unfading, kept in heaven. ## OT Antecedents - **Num 18:20** — LORD as inheritance. - **Ps 16:5-6** — The LORD is my chosen portion. - **Dan 12:2-3** — Resurrection inheritance. ## Shared structure - New birth - Living hope - Imp…

to.title: 1 Peter 2 Living Stones
to.body: # 1 Peter 2: Living Stones ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:4-8 Coming to Christ as the living stone, you yourselves like living stones are being built into a spiritual house. Psalm 118 and Isaiah 28 quoted. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 28:16** — Cornerstone in Zion — quoted. - **Ps 118:22** — Stone the builders rejected — quoted. - **Dan 2:34** — Stone cut without hands. ## Shared structure - Cornerstone - Livi…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: 2 Cor 5 Became Sin For Us
to.body: # 2 Cor 5:21: Became Sin for Us ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:21 He who knew no sin was made sin for us, that we might become the righteousness of God in him. The most compressed statement of the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16:21-22** — Scapegoat bearing iniquity. - **Isa 53:6** — LORD laid on him the iniquity of us all. - **Num 19** — Cleansing through death of clean offering. ## Shared stru…

from.title: Ezekiel Apocrypha Themes
from.body: # 4 Ezra / Apocalyptic Hope → New Creation Theology Backdrop ## OT Story — 2 Esdras (4 Ezra) 7 4 Ezra wrestles with theodicy, exile, and resurrection — featuring the Messiah's reign, new age dawning, and consolation of the righteous. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 8:18-25: Sufferings of this present time vs. coming glory. - 2 Corinthians 4:17: Light affliction working eternal glory. - Revelation 21:1-…

to.title: Isaiah 65 New Heavens
to.body: # Isaiah 65 / New Heavens and New Earth → Final Renewal ## OT Story — Isaiah 65:17-25 'I create new heavens and a new earth; the former things shall not be remembered. Be glad and rejoice forever… no more weeping… wolf and lamb graze together.' ## NT Fulfillment - 2 Peter 3:13: According to his promise we are waiting for new heavens and a new earth. - Revelation 21:1-4: A new heaven and a new eart…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Two Goats Yom Kippur
to.body: # Two Goats of Atonement → Christ's Death and Bearing-Away ## OT Story — Leviticus 16:7-22 On Yom Kippur, two goats: one slaughtered as sin offering, its blood taken behind the veil; the other (the scapegoat) bears confessed sins into the wilderness, never to return. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:11-14: Christ entered the holy places by his own blood. - Hebrews 10:11-14: One offering for sins fore…

from.title: Empty Tomb
from.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

to.title: Hosea Three Days Raised Up
to.body: # Hosea 6 / On the Third Day He Will Raise Us → Resurrection on the Third Day ## OT Story — Hosea 6:1-3 'Come, let us return to the LORD; for he has torn us, and he will heal us. After two days he will revive us; on the third day he will raise us up, that we may live before him.' ## NT Fulfillment - 1 Corinthians 15:4: Raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures. - Luke 24:46: It is …

from.title: Isaiah 9 Child Is Born
from.body: # Isaiah 9 / 'For Unto Us a Child Is Born' → Eternal King ## OT Story — Isaiah 9:6-7 'A child is born, a son is given; the government shall be upon his shoulder. His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:32-33: He will be great and called Son of the Most High; his kingdom no end. - Luke 2:11: Born this day a Savior, C…

to.title: Psalm 72 Royal Reign
to.body: # Psalm 72 / Solomon's Reign → Messiah's Universal Just Reign ## OT Story — Psalm 72 A psalm of Solomon: he judges with righteousness, defends the poor, kings of Sheba bring gifts, all nations are blessed in him, his name endures forever. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 2:11: Magi bring gold, frankincense, myrrh — echo of Sheba. - Revelation 21:24-26: Kings bring glory into the city. - Galatians 3:14:…

from.title: Light Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Light Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1; Rev 21 Genesis's first creative word is 'let there be light'; the prophets promise light to the nations; Christ is the Light of the World; the New Jerusalem needs no lamp. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:3** — Let there be light. - **Isa 9:2** — People in darkness have seen great light. - **Isa 60:1-3** — Arise, shine. ## Shared structure - Creation li…

to.title: Sons Of Light DSS
to.body: # Sons of Light vs. Sons of Darkness → Pauline Light Ethics ## OT Story — 1QM, 1QS (Dead Sea Scrolls) Qumran's literature pits 'sons of light' against 'sons of darkness' in a cosmic and ethical dualism awaiting eschatological battle. ## NT Fulfillment - Ephesians 5:8: You were once darkness, now light in the Lord — walk as children of light. - 1 Thessalonians 5:5: You are all children of light. - …

from.title: amnos
from.body: # amnos (ἀμνός) — lamb ## Semantic field A lamb — used four times in NT, all christologically. Distinct from arnion (Revelation's word). ## Theological weight "Behold the Amnos of God who takes away the sin of the world" (John 1:29). Acts 8 applies Isaiah 53's amnos-led-to-slaughter to Christ. 1 Peter 1:19 names His "precious blood as of an unblemished amnos." ## Septuagint mapping Renders Hebrew …

to.title: arnion
to.body: # arnion (ἀρνίον) — little lamb ## Semantic field A small lamb. Used 29 times in Revelation for Christ — the slain-yet-standing lamb on the throne. ## Theological weight The arnion in Revelation embodies paradox — slain lamb yet sovereign judge, weak yet conquering, sacrificial yet enthroned. The whole christology of the Apocalypse rides on this image. ## Septuagint mapping Used for various small-…

from.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

to.title: Showbread Twelve Loaves
to.body: # Bread of the Presence / Twelve Loaves → Christ and His Twelve ## OT Story — Exodus 25:30; Leviticus 24:5-9 Twelve loaves sit on a gold table in the holy place 'before the LORD always.' Priests eat them weekly in the holy place. ## NT Fulfillment - John 6:35: I am the bread of life. - 1 Corinthians 10:17: We who are many are one bread, one body. - Mark 2:25-26: David's eating the showbread antici…

from.title: Psalm 110 Priest King
from.body: # Psalm 110 / 'Sit at My Right Hand' → Priest-King Forever ## OT Story — Psalm 110 'The LORD says to my Lord: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool… You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 22:41-46: Jesus' messianic riddle from this psalm. - Hebrews 5:6; 7:1-28: Christ's eternal priesthood after Melchizedek. - Acts 2:34-36: Pete…

to.title: Psalm 23 Shepherd
to.body: # Psalm 23 / The LORD My Shepherd → Christ the Good Shepherd ## OT Story — Psalm 23 'The LORD is my shepherd, I shall not want… he restores my soul… valley of the shadow of death… your rod and staff comfort me… you prepare a table before me.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 10:11: I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd lays down his life. - Revelation 7:17: The Lamb at the center of the throne will sh…

from.title: Absalom Rebellion
from.body: # Absalom's Rebellion → Son Rebels Against Father; Cross Subverts ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 15-18 Absalom steals the people's hearts, drives David from Jerusalem. David crosses the Kidron weeping, ascending the Mount of Olives. Eventually Absalom dies hanging in a tree. ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 19:14: 'We do not want this man to reign over us.' - John 18:1: Jesus crossed the Kidron — same path as Dav…

to.title: Bathsheba Uriah Sin
to.body: # David's Sin with Bathsheba → Royal Failure and Atoning Need ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 11 King David, idle in spring war-season, takes Bathsheba; she conceives. To cover it, he orders Uriah's death in battle. The thing he had done displeased the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Psalm 51: David's penitential prayer. - 1 John 1:9: If we confess, he is faithful and just to forgive. - Matthew 1:6: Listed in ge…

from.title: Revelation 1 Alpha Omega
from.body: # Revelation 1: Alpha and Omega ## NT Text — Rev 1:8 The Lord declares himself 'the Alpha and the Omega, who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.' Isaianic divine-name claims gather here. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 41:4** — I, the LORD, the first and with the last. - **Isa 44:6** — I am the first and I am the last. - **Isa 48:12** — I am he; I am the first; I am also the last. ## Shared…

to.title: Revelation 1 Son Of Man Vision
to.body: # Revelation 1: Son of Man Vision ## NT Text — Rev 1:12-18 John sees one 'like a son of man' among golden lampstands — eyes like fire, feet like burnished bronze, voice like many waters. Daniel 7 and 10 are gathered. ## OT Antecedents - **Dan 7:13** — One like a son of man. - **Dan 10:5-6** — Man clothed in linen, eyes like fire. - **Ezek 1:7** — Feet like burnished bronze. ## Shared structure - A…

from.title: Cleansing Of Temple
from.body: # Cleansing of the Temple ## NT Text — Matt 21:12-13; John 2:13-22 Jesus drives out the money-changers, quoting Isaiah and Jeremiah. The act is prophetic sign-theater fulfilling Malachi's promise that the Lord would suddenly come to his temple. ## OT Antecedents - **Mal 3:1-3** — The Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple. - **Isa 56:7** — My house shall be a house of prayer for all n…

to.title: Ezekiel 10 Glory Departs
to.body: # Glory Departs the Temple → Christ Departs the Temple ## OT Story — Ezekiel 10-11 The glory of the LORD lifts from the cherubim, moves to the threshold, and finally to the mountain east of the city. The temple is left desolate before destruction. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:38-24:2: 'Your house is left to you desolate'; not one stone left. - Luke 21:5-6: Foretelling the temple's fall. - Mark 1…

from.title: Words From Cross Eli Eli
from.body: # Word from Cross: Eli Eli Lama Sabachthani ## NT Text — Matt 27:46; Mark 15:34 Jesus cries Psalm 22's opening. By invoking the incipit he invokes the whole psalm — its agony and its vindication. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 22:1** — Why have you forsaken me? - **Lam 1:12** — Is it nothing to you? - **Isa 53:10** — It pleased the LORD to crush him. ## Shared structure - Forsakenness expressed - Psalm …

to.title: Words From Cross Finished
to.body: # Word from Cross: It Is Finished ## NT Text — John 19:30 Jesus cries 'tetelestai' — accomplished. The single word echoes Genesis 2's creation completion and Exodus 39's tabernacle completion. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 2:1-3** — Heavens and earth finished. - **Exod 39:32** — Work of tabernacle finished. - **Isa 53:11** — By his knowledge make many righteous. ## Shared structure - Completion declar…

from.title: Caleb Faithful
from.body: # Caleb Wholly Followed the LORD → Persevering Faith Inherits ## OT Story — Joshua 14:6-15 Forty-five years after the spy mission, eighty-five-year-old Caleb claims his portion: 'Give me this hill country.' He still wholly follows the LORD. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 6:11-12: Through faith and patience inherit the promises. - Revelation 2:10: Be faithful unto death; I will give the crown of life.…

to.title: Spies Twelve
to.body: # Twelve Spies / Two Faithful → Witnesses of the Promise ## OT Story — Numbers 13 Twelve spies survey Canaan. Ten report giants; two — Caleb and Joshua — say, 'We can surely go up.' Israel believes the ten and forfeits the land for forty years. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 3:16-19: They could not enter because of unbelief. - Hebrews 11:30-31: Faith brought down Jericho. - Luke 10:1: Two witnesses s…

from.title: Romans 6 Baptized Into Death
from.body: # Romans 6: Baptized into His Death ## NT Text — Rom 6:1-11 Believers are baptized into Christ's death and raised to walk in newness of life. Baptism is union with the Death-and-Resurrection of Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage as death/resurrection of Israel. - **Josh 3** — Jordan crossing into the land. - **Ezek 36:25-27** — Sprinkled with clean water; new spirit. ## Shar…

to.title: Water Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Water Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 4; John 7; Rev 22 Creation's waters, flood, Red Sea, wilderness rocks, Jordan, the Samaritan well, baptism, the river of life — water marks judgment and life. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 1:2** — Spirit hovering over waters. - **Exod 14** — Red Sea passage. - **Ezek 47** — River from the temple. ## Shared structure - Chaos-water - Judgment-water - Delive…

from.title: aletheia
from.body: # aletheia (ἀλήθεια) — truth ## Semantic field Literally "non-concealment" — that which is unhidden, real. In John especially carries the Hebrew connotation of emet — faithful reliability — alongside Greek "correspondence" sense. ## Theological weight "I am the way, the aletheia, and the life" (John 14:6). Jesus is not only a true teacher but the embodiment of aletheia. The Spirit is the Pneuma of…

to.title: emet
to.body: # emet (אֱמֶת) — truth / faithfulness ## Semantic field Firmness, reliability, that which can be trusted because it conforms to reality. Frequently paired with chesed (Ex 34:6) to express God's reliable covenant love. ## Theological weight Emet is not abstract truth-value but the trustworthiness of God's character and word. To be "in emet" is to be solid, dependable, real. ## Septuagint mapping Us…

from.title: Sower
from.body: # The Sower ## What happens A sower scatters seed that falls on path, rocks, thorns, and good soil — yielding only where the soil receives. Jesus interprets the seed as the word and the soils as kinds of hearers. ## Typological significance Foundational kingdom parable: the word is the active agent, and the receptive heart is the variable. Establishes that the kingdom advances by sowing — not coer…

to.title: Wheat And Tares
to.body: # The Wheat and the Tares ## What happens An enemy sows tares among a man's wheat at night; the master forbids early uprooting and orders separation at the harvest. ## Typological significance Eschatological patience: the kingdom and the counter-kingdom coexist in the present field, with judgment deferred to the harvest. Restrains premature purges and frames history as a contested sowing. ## Conne…

from.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
from.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

to.title: Revelation 5 Slain Lamb Worthy
to.body: # Revelation 5: Slain Lamb Worthy ## NT Text — Rev 5:6-14 Between the throne and the elders stood a Lamb as though slain. The Lion of Judah is also the slain-yet-standing Lamb. He alone is worthy to open the scroll. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49:9-10** — Lion of Judah. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Lamb led to slaughter. ## Shared structure - Lamb-as-slain - Standing posture - Sev…

from.title: Crown Of Thorns Curse Reversed
from.body: # Crown of Thorns: Curse Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 27:29; John 19:2 Soldiers crown Jesus with thorns. The symbol of Adam's curse becomes the messianic crown — the king bears the curse on his head. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:17-18** — Thorns and thistles as curse. - **Num 33:55** — Thorns in your sides. - **Ezek 28:24** — No more brier or thorn for Israel. ## Shared structure - Curse-symbol - Roy…

to.title: Sweat As Blood
to.body: # Sweat as Blood ## NT Text — Luke 22:44 Luke records that Jesus' sweat became 'like great drops of blood' in Gethsemane. The detail echoes Adam's curse of toilsome sweat and anticipates the cross's bloodshed; the new Adam labors in the garden of obedience. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:19** — By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread. - **Lev 17:11** — Life is in the blood. - **Ps 22:14** — I a…

from.title: Words From Cross I Thirst
from.body: # Word from Cross: I Thirst ## NT Text — John 19:28 Jesus says 'I thirst' to fulfill Scripture. The Bread-of-Life is consumed by thirst that he might quench ours. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 69:21** — Vinegar for my thirst. - **Ps 22:15** — Tongue cleaves. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul makes offering for sin. ## Shared structure - Thirst expressed - Sour wine offered - Fulfillment formula - Reversal ## Theo…

to.title: Words From Cross Into Your Hands
to.body: # Word from Cross: Into Your Hands I Commit ## NT Text — Luke 23:46 Jesus dies praying Psalm 31:5 — a Davidic prayer of trust. Stephen later echoes it. Death is recast as commendation. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 31:5** — Into your hand I commit my spirit. - **Eccl 12:7** — Spirit returns to God. - **Acts 7:59** — Stephen echoes this prayer. ## Shared structure - Spirit-commendation - Trust in Father…

from.title: Crown Of Thorns Curse Reversed
from.body: # Crown of Thorns: Curse Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 27:29; John 19:2 Soldiers crown Jesus with thorns. The symbol of Adam's curse becomes the messianic crown — the king bears the curse on his head. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:17-18** — Thorns and thistles as curse. - **Num 33:55** — Thorns in your sides. - **Ezek 28:24** — No more brier or thorn for Israel. ## Shared structure - Curse-symbol - Roy…

to.title: Galatians 3 Curse Of Law
to.body: # Galatians 3: Curse of the Law ## NT Text — Gal 3:10-14 Christ became a curse for us, hanging on a tree — fulfilling Deut 21:23 and Deut 27:26. The curse of the law is borne so that Abrahamic blessing flows to the nations. ## OT Antecedents - **Deut 21:23** — Cursed is everyone hanged on a tree. - **Deut 27:26** — Cursed is anyone who does not uphold this law. - **Gen 12:3** — Abrahamic blessing …

from.title: New Covenant Jeremiah 31
from.body: # New Covenant (Jeremiah 31) ## The oracle "Behold, the days are coming… when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and the house of Judah — not like the covenant I made with their fathers" — law written on the heart, sins remembered no more. ## Date / context Jeremiah just before/during the fall of Jerusalem, c. 587 BC. ## Fulfillment Jesus inaugurates the new covenant in his blood …

to.title: New Heart Ezekiel 36
to.body: # New Heart (Ezekiel 36) ## The oracle "I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you shall be clean… A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you… and I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes." ## Date / context Ezekiel's restoration oracle to the exiles. ## Fulfillment Jesus assumes it as common knowledge with Nicodemus ("born of water and Spirit…

from.title: Isaiah 49 Second Servant Song
from.body: # Second Servant Song / Light to Nations → Christ to the Gentiles ## OT Story — Isaiah 49:1-13 'The LORD called me from the womb… It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob; I will make you a light for the nations.' ## NT Fulfillment - Acts 13:47: Paul cites it as the apostolic commission. - Luke 2:30-32: Simeon — 'a light for revelation to the Gentiles.'…

to.title: Isaiah 50 Third Servant Song
to.body: # Third Servant Song / I Gave My Back → Christ Set His Face ## OT Story — Isaiah 50:4-9 'I gave my back to those who strike, my cheeks to those who pull out the beard… I have set my face like a flint; I know I shall not be put to shame.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 9:51: He set his face to go to Jerusalem. - Matthew 26:67: They spat in his face and struck him. - Matthew 27:14: He gave no answer. ## S…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 Royal Priesthood
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: Royal Priesthood ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:9-10 You are a chosen race, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for his possession — Exodus 19:6 reapplied to the church. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19:5-6** — Kingdom of priests, holy nation. - **Hos 1:9-10; 2:23** — Not-my-people now my people. - **Isa 43:21** — People formed for myself. ## Shared structure - Exodus identity titles - Hosea rev…

to.title: 1 Peter 5 Chief Shepherd
to.body: # 1 Peter 5: Chief Shepherd ## NT Text — 1 Pet 5:1-4 Elders shepherd the flock; when the Chief Shepherd appears, they will receive the unfading crown of glory. Davidic-shepherd typology gathered into Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 34:23** — My servant David — one shepherd. - **Ps 23** — The LORD is my shepherd. - **Zech 11:7-17** — Two shepherd-staffs. ## Shared structure - Under-shepherds - C…

from.title: 1 Peter 1 Born Anew
from.body: # 1 Peter 1: Born Anew through Living Word ## NT Text — 1 Pet 1:22-25 Born again through the living and abiding word of God — Isaiah's 'grass withers, the word stands forever' cited as charter. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 40:6-8** — Grass withers, word stands forever. - **Ezek 36:26** — New heart and Spirit. - **Ps 119:25** — Give me life according to your word. ## Shared structure - New birth - Liv…

to.title: Ezekiel 36 New Heart
to.body: # Ezekiel 36 / New Heart, New Spirit → Born of Water and Spirit ## OT Story — Ezekiel 36:25-27 'I will sprinkle clean water on you… A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you. I will remove the heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 3:5-8: Born of water and Spirit. - Titus 3:5: Washing of regeneration and renewal of the Holy Spirit. - 2 Cor…

from.title: Hebrews 11 Cloud Of Witnesses
from.body: # Hebrews 11: Cloud of Witnesses ## NT Text — Heb 11:1-12:1 A roll-call of faith — Abel, Enoch, Noah, Abraham, Sarah, Moses, Rahab, and more — culminates in 'such a great cloud of witnesses.' Faith looks forward to the same promise we possess. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4-25** — Patriarchal exemplars. - **Exod** — Mosaic figures. - **2 Kgs 2** — Elijah taken up. ## Shared structure - Definition of …

to.title: James 2 Faith And Works
to.body: # James 2: Faith and Works ## NT Text — James 2:14-26 Faith without works is dead. James cites Abraham offering Isaac and Rahab hiding the spies — both vindicated by deeds that proved their faith. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 22** — Abraham's offering of Isaac. - **Josh 2** — Rahab and the spies. - **Gen 15:6** — Abraham believed God, counted as righteousness. ## Shared structure - Faith confessed - …

from.title: Daniel 7 Son Of Man
from.body: # Daniel 7 Son of Man → Jesus' Self-Identification ## OT Story — Daniel 7:9-14 Daniel sees a night vision. Four monstrous beasts arise from the chaotic sea; they represent earthly empires. Then the scene shifts to a heavenly throne room: *"As I looked, thrones were placed, and the **Ancient of Days** took his seat ... the court sat in judgment, and the books were opened ... and behold, with the cl…

to.title: Son Of Man
to.body: # Son of Man Jesus' favored self-designation — drawing on Daniel 7:13-14's heavenly figure who receives dominion from the Ancient of Days, while also evoking Ezekiel's prophetic "son of man" address. ## Key passages - Daniel 7:13-14 — the heavenly Son of Man - Ezekiel 2:1 (and ~93x) — prophetic address - Mark 2:10, 28; 8:31; 14:62 — Jesus' self-use - Matthew 25:31 — Son of Man on his throne - Acts…

from.title: Jeremiah 31 New Covenant
from.body: # Jeremiah 31 / New Covenant → Christ's Blood Inaugurates It ## OT Story — Jeremiah 31:31-34 'Behold, the days are coming when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel… I will put my law within them, write it on their hearts, and forgive their iniquity.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 22:20: This cup is the new covenant in my blood. - Hebrews 8:6-13: Christ's covenant is established on better…

to.title: Stone Tablets Heart Tablets
to.body: # Stone Tablets → Tablets of the Heart ## OT Story — Exodus 31:18; 34:1 God writes the Ten Words on stone tablets at Sinai. The first set is shattered; the second carried into the ark. ## NT Fulfillment - 2 Corinthians 3:3: A letter from Christ, written not on stone but on tablets of human hearts. - Jeremiah 31:33: I will write my law on their hearts. - Ezekiel 36:26: A new heart, a heart of flesh…

from.title: Calling Of Twelve
from.body: # Calling of the Twelve ## NT Text — Mark 3:13-19; Luke 6:12-16 Jesus ascends a mountain and appoints twelve apostles. The number is constitutive: a new Israel is being formed around a new lawgiver. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 49** — Twelve patriarchs. - **Exod 24:4** — Twelve pillars at Sinai. - **Josh 4:1-9** — Twelve stones from the Jordan. ## Shared structure - Twelve representatives - Mountain …

Israel believes the ten and forfeits the land for forty years. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 3:16-19: They could not enter because of unbelief. - Hebrews 11:30-31: Faith brought down Jericho. - Luke 10:1: Two witnesses s…

from.title: Revelation 20 Millennium
from.body: # Revelation 20: The Millennium ## NT Text — Rev 20:1-6 The dragon is bound; the saints reign with Christ a thousand years. A debated vision of the church-age or future kingdom. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 90:4** — Thousand years as one day. - **Isa 65:18-25** — Long-lived peaceful kingdom. - **Ezek 37:24-28** — David shepherd-king of restored Israel. ## Shared structure - Dragon bound - First resurr…

to.title: Revelation 4 Throne Vision
to.body: # Revelation 4: Throne Vision ## NT Text — Rev 4 John sees the throne with the rainbow, four living creatures, twenty-four elders — gathering Ezekiel 1, Isaiah 6, and Daniel 7 into one vision. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 1** — Four living creatures around the throne. - **Isa 6** — Seraphim and Holy, Holy, Holy. - **Dan 7:9-10** — Ancient of Days enthroned. ## Shared structure - Heavenly throne - Su…

from.title: Hebrews 12 Mount Zion Not Sinai
from.body: # Hebrews 12: Mount Zion, Not Sinai ## NT Text — Heb 12:18-24 You have not come to Sinai with its terror but to Mount Zion, to the heavenly Jerusalem, to angels, to the assembly of the firstborn, to Jesus the mediator. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 19** — Sinai theophany of terror. - **Ps 48** — Zion's beauty. - **Isa 60** — Zion glorified. ## Shared structure - Sinai/terror contrast - Zion/grace des…

to.title: Hebrews 9 Once For All
to.body: # Hebrews 9: Once for All ## NT Text — Heb 9:11-14, 24-28 Christ entered the heavenly sanctuary once for all by his own blood. Annual atonement is superseded by single, eternal redemption. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement — annual. - **Exod 26:33** — Veil before the most-holy. - **Zech 9:11** — Blood of the covenant. ## Shared structure - Heavenly sanctuary - Once-for-all entry - …

from.title: Crossing Jordan On Dry Ground
from.body: # Crossing the Jordan → Entry into Promised Rest ## OT Story — Joshua 3 The priests carrying the ark step into the Jordan at flood; the waters stand in a heap and Israel crosses on dry ground into the land. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 6:3-4: Baptized into Christ's death, raised to walk in newness. - Hebrews 4:8-11: Joshua did not give them final rest; a Sabbath remains. - Matthew 3:13-17: Jesus' ba…

from.title: 1 Cor 10 Rock Was Christ
from.body: # 1 Cor 10: The Rock Was Christ ## NT Text — 1 Cor 10:1-13 Paul reads Israel's wilderness experiences typologically: cloud, sea, spiritual food and drink — 'and the Rock was Christ.' Sacrament-shaped warning. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 14** — Cloud and sea. - **Exod 17** — Water from the rock. - **Num 20** — Second rock-incident. ## Shared structure - Cloud/sea as baptism - Manna as spiritual food…

to.title: 1 Cor 5 Christ Our Passover
to.body: # 1 Cor 5: Christ Our Passover ## NT Text — 1 Cor 5:7-8 Paul calls Christ 'our Passover lamb, sacrificed for us,' and exhorts cleansing of old leaven. Passover and Unleavened Bread become ethical patterns. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover ordinance. - **Exod 13:6-7** — Unleavened bread for seven days. - **Deut 16:1-8** — Passover-Unleavened Bread combined. ## Shared structure - Lamb slai…

from.title: Feast Throughout Scripture
from.body: # Feast Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — Matt 22; Luke 14; Rev 19 From Passover to Sinai's covenant meal, to Isaiah 25's feast on the mountain, to the Last Supper, to the marriage supper — feasts are eschatological signs. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12** — Passover meal. - **Exod 24:9-11** — Covenant meal on Sinai. - **Isa 25:6** — Feast of fat things on this mountain. ## Shared structure - Shared…

to.title: Lamb Throughout Scripture
to.body: # Lamb Throughout Scripture ## NT Text — John 1:29; Rev 5 Abel's firstborn, Abraham's substitute, the Passover lamb, the daily tamid, Isaiah's silent lamb, John's 'Lamb of God', Revelation's slain Lamb. The lamb is canon's central animal. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 4:4** — Abel's firstborn lamb. - **Gen 22:8** — God will provide himself a lamb. - **Exod 12** — Passover lamb. - **Isa 53:7** — Like a…

from.title: Joseph Sold By Brothers
from.body: # Joseph Sold by His Brothers → The Betrayal and Vindication of Christ ## OT Story — Genesis 37, 40, 41-50 Joseph is the **beloved son** of Jacob, given a special garment (the "coat of many colors"). He has prophetic dreams in which his brothers and parents bow down to him. His brothers hate him and plot to kill him; instead, on Judah's suggestion, they sell him for **twenty pieces of silver** to …

to.title: Joseph Provides Bread
to.body: # Joseph Provides Bread in Famine → Christ the Bread of Life ## OT Story — Genesis 41:53-57 Famine spreads over all the earth. Joseph alone has bread. 'Go to Joseph,' Pharaoh says, 'and do what he tells you.' Nations come to Egypt to be fed. ## NT Fulfillment - John 6:35: I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger. - John 2:5: Mary's 'do whatever he tells you' echoes Pharaoh's 'g…

from.title: kyrios
from.body: # kyrios (κύριος) — lord ## Semantic field Lord, master, owner. Used for ordinary masters but also as the LXX rendering of YHWH and Adonai. ## Theological weight "Iesous Kyrios" — "Jesus is Lord" — is the earliest Christian confession (Rom 10:9). Because kyrios translates YHWH in LXX, the confession is implicit assertion of Jesus' divine identity. ## Septuagint mapping Renders both YHWH and adon. …

to.title: yhwh
to.body: # yhwh (יהוה) — the LORD ## Semantic field The tetragrammaton, the personal covenant name of God revealed at the burning bush. Related to the verb "to be" — "I AM WHO I AM" / "I will be who I will be." Read aloud as Adonai in Jewish tradition. ## Theological weight YHWH is the proper name of the God of Israel — not a generic deity but the One who covenants, delivers from Egypt, and dwells with His…

from.title: Joel Spirit Poured Out

to.body: # Psalm 118 / Stone the Builders Rejected → Cornerstone ## OT Story — Psalm 118:22-23 'The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. This is the LORD's doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:42: Jesus applies it to himself. - Acts 4:11: Peter to the Sanhedrin — you are the builders. - 1 Peter 2:7: Cornerstone for believers, stumbling-stone for unbelievers…

from.title: Isaiah 9 Child Is Born
from.body: # Isaiah 9 / 'For Unto Us a Child Is Born' → Eternal King ## OT Story — Isaiah 9:6-7 'A child is born, a son is given; the government shall be upon his shoulder. His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:32-33: He will be great and called Son of the Most High; his kingdom no end. - Luke 2:11: Born this day a Savior, C…

to.title: Psalm 8 Son Of Man
to.body: # Psalm 8 / Son of Man Crowned → Christ's Dominion Restored ## OT Story — Psalm 8 'What is man that you are mindful of him, the son of man that you care for him?' Made a little lower than the heavenly beings, crowned with glory, all things under his feet. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 2:5-9: Applied to Jesus — we see Jesus crowned with glory and honor. - 1 Corinthians 15:27: All things put in subjec…

from.title: Jonah Three Days
from.body: # Jonah Three Days in the Fish → Three Days in the Tomb This typology is **the only one Jesus himself names explicitly as a sign**. It is therefore exceptionally load-bearing — if any typology is methodologically validated by canonical scripture, this one is. ## OT Story — Jonah 1-2 God calls Jonah to preach repentance to Nineveh, the capital of Israel's enemy Assyria. Jonah flees by ship in the o…

to.title: Bronze Serpent
to.body: # The Bronze Serpent → "The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up" One of the typologies **Jesus himself names explicitly**. As with Jonah (Mt 12:40), Jesus' own use of this OT story authorizes its typological reading and tells us exactly how he wants it understood. ## OT Story — Numbers 21:4-9 Israel grumbles against God during the wilderness journey. God sends "fiery serpents" whose bite kills many. The …

from.title: Revelation 3 Open Door
from.body: # Revelation 3: Open Door ## NT Text — Rev 3:7-8 To Philadelphia: 'I have set before you an open door which no one can shut.' Davidic-key access from Isaiah 22 applied to Christ. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 22:22** — Key of David — open and none shall shut. - **1 Kgs 22:17** — Sheep without shepherd context. - **Ps 118:19-20** — Open to me the gates of righteousness. ## Shared structure - Key of Dav…

to.title: Revelation 5 Scroll And Seals
to.body: # Revelation 5: Scroll and Seven Seals ## NT Text — Rev 5:1-7 A scroll sealed with seven seals — none worthy to open. The Lamb takes it. Daniel's sealed words and Ezekiel's scroll converge. ## OT Antecedents - **Ezek 2:9-3:3** — Scroll given to the prophet. - **Dan 12:4** — Seal the book until the time of the end. - **Isa 29:11** — Words of a sealed book. ## Shared structure - Sealed scroll - Sear…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Absalom Hung On Tree
to.body: # Absalom Hangs in a Tree → Cursed One on the Tree ## OT Story — 2 Samuel 18:9-15 Absalom's hair catches in the branches of a great oak; he hangs between heaven and earth while his mule rides on. Joab pierces him with three darts. ## NT Fulfillment - Galatians 3:13: Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree. - Deuteronomy 21:22-23: A hanged man is cursed by God. - John 19:34: Pierced in the side. ## …

from.title: Cosmic Signs
from.body: # Cosmic Signs ## The oracle Before the Day of YHWH, wonders appear in heaven and on earth — sun darkened, moon to blood, stars falling, heavens rolled like a scroll. ## Date / context Recurring prophetic topos from Isaiah through Joel into apocalyptic. ## Fulfillment Jesus appropriates the imagery for his return (Matthew 24:29); Revelation 6 weaves it into the sixth seal. Initial fulfillment may …

to.title: Olivet Discourse
to.body: # Olivet Discourse ## The oracle On the Mount of Olives, Jesus answers about the temple's destruction and his coming: wars, false messiahs, persecutions, abomination of desolation, tribulation, cosmic signs, and the Son of Man coming on the clouds. ## Date / context Holy Week, c. AD 30, looking at the Second Temple from across the Kidron. ## Fulfillment AD 70 fall of Jerusalem fulfills the near-ho…

from.title: Revelation 16 Bowls Of Wrath
from.body: # Revelation 16: Bowls of Wrath ## NT Text — Rev 16 Seven bowls of wrath are poured — sores, blood, scorching sun, darkness, Euphrates dried. Egyptian plagues climax in cosmic recapitulation. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 7-12** — Egyptian plagues. - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of wrath drained. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to nations. ## Shared structure - Bowls poured - Seven completes - Plague-…

to.title: Revelation 9 Bottomless Pit
to.body: # Revelation 9: Bottomless Pit ## NT Text — Rev 9:1-12 A star fallen opens the bottomless pit; locust-scorpions emerge. Joel's locust army and Genesis 1's tehom find their dark fulfillment. ## OT Antecedents - **Joel 2:4-10** — Locust army. - **Exod 10** — Locust plague. - **Gen 1:2** — Deep (tehom). ## Shared structure - Pit opened - Locust-scorpions - Five months torment - Apollyon/Abaddon king …

from.title: Mercy Seat Atonement
from.body: # Mercy Seat / Hilasterion → Christ Our Propitiation ## OT Story — Exodus 25:17-22; Leviticus 16:14-15 Over the ark sits the kapporet, the mercy seat, flanked by two cherubim. Once a year the high priest sprinkles blood there for the sins of the people. ## NT Fulfillment - Romans 3:25: God put forward Christ as a propitiation (hilasterion). - Hebrews 9:5: The cherubim of glory overshadowing the me…

to.title: Tabernacle Pattern From Heaven
to.body: # Tabernacle Built to Heavenly Pattern → Heavenly Sanctuary ## OT Story — Exodus 25:9; 25:40 Moses is to build the tabernacle 'according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.' The earthly is a copy of the heavenly. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 8:5: They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. - Hebrews 9:23-24: Christ entered the true sanctuary, of which the earthly is a copy. - Revelat…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Sacrifice Of Red Heifer
to.body: # Ashes of the Red Heifer Stored → Continual Cleansing ## OT Story — Numbers 19:1-10 The ashes of the red heifer are stored outside the camp 'for the water of impurity' — used whenever a person becomes defiled by death. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 9:13-14: If the ashes purify the flesh, how much more the blood of Christ. - 1 John 1:9: He is faithful and just to cleanse. - Titus 3:5: The washing of…

from.title: Crown Of Thorns Curse Reversed
from.body: # Crown of Thorns: Curse Reversed ## NT Text — Matt 27:29; John 19:2 Soldiers crown Jesus with thorns. The symbol of Adam's curse becomes the messianic crown — the king bears the curse on his head. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:17-18** — Thorns and thistles as curse. - **Num 33:55** — Thorns in your sides. - **Ezek 28:24** — No more brier or thorn for Israel. ## Shared structure - Curse-symbol - Roy…

to.title: Cup Of Cup Of Wrath
to.body: # Cup of the Lord's Wrath ## NT Text — Matt 26:39; Mark 14:36 Jesus prays that the 'cup' pass from him. OT 'cup' imagery is consistently the cup of God's wrath against sin. Christ drinks what his people deserved. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 51:17** — Cup of his wrath, the bowl of staggering. - **Jer 25:15-29** — Cup of wrath given to the nations. - **Ps 75:8** — Cup with foaming wine. ## Shared stru…

from.title: Solomon Wisdom Request
from.body: # Solomon Asks for Wisdom → One Greater Than Solomon ## OT Story — 1 Kings 3:5-15 God appears to Solomon at Gibeon. Solomon asks for a hearing heart to judge the people. God grants wisdom beyond measure, plus riches and honor. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 12:42: Something greater than Solomon is here. - 1 Corinthians 1:30: Christ became to us wisdom from God. - Colossians 2:3: In him are hidden all…

to.title: Wisdom Calls At Gates
to.body: # Wisdom Calls at the Gates → Christ Pleads with the City ## OT Story — Proverbs 1:20-33; 8:1-11 Wisdom raises her voice in the streets, at the gates, in the public square: 'How long, simple ones, will you love simplicity?' She offers life; refusal brings calamity. ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 23:37: How often I would have gathered you, but you would not. - Luke 13:34: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem. - Rev…

from.title: 1 John 2 Atoning Sacrifice
from.body: # 1 John 2: Atoning Sacrifice ## NT Text — 1 John 2:1-2 If anyone sins we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous. He is the propitiation for our sins — and for the whole world. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement propitiation. - **Isa 53:10** — Soul an offering for sin. - **Ps 32:1-2** — Blessed is he whose sin is covered. ## Shared structure - Advocate - Propit…

to.title: Coats Of Skin First Sacrifice
to.body: # Coats of Skin → Garments of Salvation ## OT Story — Genesis 3:21 After the fall, Adam and Eve cover themselves with fig leaves. God himself makes garments of skin and clothes them. Implicit in the act is the death of an animal — the first sacrifice and covering. ## NT Fulfillment - Isaiah 61:10: He has clothed me with the garments of salvation, covered me with the robe of righteousness. - Romans…

from.title: Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant
from.body: # Isaiah 53 Suffering Servant → Christ's Passion ## OT Story — Isaiah 52:13-53:12 The fourth and climactic Servant Song. The Servant is *"despised and rejected"* (53:3), *"pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities"* (53:5). He is silent before his accusers — *"like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth"…

to.title: Psalm 40 Body Prepared
to.body: # Psalm 40 / 'A Body You Have Prepared' → Incarnation for Sacrifice ## OT Story — Psalm 40:6-8 'Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you have prepared for me… Behold, I have come to do your will, O God.' The psalmist offers obedience above ritual. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 10:5-10: Applied directly to Christ entering the world. - Philippians 2:7-8: He emptied himself, obedient t…

from.title: 1 John 1 Walk In Light
from.body: # 1 John 1: Walk in the Light ## NT Text — 1 John 1:5-10 God is light; in him is no darkness. If we walk in the light we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus cleanses us from all sin. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 27:1** — The LORD is my light. - **Isa 60:1** — Arise, shine, for your light has come. - **Mic 7:8** — Though I sit in darkness, the LORD will be my light. ## Shared struc…

to.title: 2 Cor 5 Reconciliation Through Christ
to.body: # 2 Cor 5: Reconciliation through Christ ## NT Text — 2 Cor 5:18-21 God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not counting trespasses. He made him to be sin for us — the great exchange. ## OT Antecedents - **Lev 16** — Day of Atonement bearing of iniquity. - **Isa 53:5-6** — Iniquity of us all laid on him. - **Hos 6:1** — He has torn that he may heal — reconciliation pattern. ## Shared s…

from.title: Ezekiel 36 New Heart
from.body: # Ezekiel 36 / New Heart, New Spirit → Born of Water and Spirit ## OT Story — Ezekiel 36:25-27 'I will sprinkle clean water on you… A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you. I will remove the heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.' ## NT Fulfillment - John 3:5-8: Born of water and Spirit. - Titus 3:5: Washing of regeneration and renewal of the Holy Spirit. - 2 Cor…

to.title: Pierced Side Blood And Water
to.body: # Pierced Side: Blood and Water ## NT Text — John 19:34-37 A soldier pierces Jesus' side; blood and water flow. John cites Zechariah 12:10. ## OT Antecedents - **Zech 12:10** — They will look on him whom they pierced. - **Exod 17:6** — Water from struck rock. - **Ezek 47:1** — Water from temple. ## Shared structure - Pierced side - Blood and water - Look-and-mourn - Temple-water echo ## Theologica…

from.title: Daniel 6 Lions Den
from.body: # Daniel in the Lions' Den → Sealed Tomb and Vindication ## OT Story — Daniel 6 Daniel prays openly; he is cast to the lions; the stone sealed with the king's signet. At dawn the king finds him alive; God shut the lions' mouths. The accusers are devoured. ## NT Fulfillment - Hebrews 11:33: By faith they stopped the mouths of lions. - 2 Timothy 4:17: I was rescued from the lion's mouth. - Matthew 2…

to.title: Empty Tomb
to.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

from.title: 1 Peter 2 He Bore Our Sins
from.body: # 1 Peter 2: He Bore Our Sins ## NT Text — 1 Pet 2:21-25 Christ left an example; he himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. Isaiah 53 is paraphrased throughout. ## OT Antecedents - **Isa 53:4-6** — He bore our griefs; by his wounds we are healed. - **Isa 53:7** — Silent like a lamb. - **Isa 53:11** — Bear their iniquities. ## Shared struct…

to.title: Sweat As Blood
to.body: # Sweat as Blood ## NT Text — Luke 22:44 Luke records that Jesus' sweat became 'like great drops of blood' in Gethsemane. The detail echoes Adam's curse of toilsome sweat and anticipates the cross's bloodshed; the new Adam labors in the garden of obedience. ## OT Antecedents - **Gen 3:19** — By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread. - **Lev 17:11** — Life is in the blood. - **Ps 22:14** — I a…

from.title: Bones Not Broken
from.body: # Bones Not Broken ## NT Text — John 19:33-36 Jesus' legs are not broken; John cites the Passover-lamb statute. The true Passover Lamb dies in conformity to the Exodus rite. ## OT Antecedents - **Exod 12:46** — Not a bone shall be broken. - **Num 9:12** — Same rule for Passover. - **Ps 34:20** — He keeps all his bones. ## Shared structure - Unbroken bones - Passover statute - Preservation amid dea…

to.title: Empty Tomb
to.body: # Empty Tomb ## NT Text — Matt 28:6; John 20:6-7 The tomb is empty; grave-clothes remain. The body is raised, not stolen. ## OT Antecedents - **Ps 16:10** — You will not abandon my soul to Sheol. - **Hos 6:2** — Third day he will raise us. - **Isa 53:10-11** — He shall see his offspring. ## Shared structure - Tomb opened - Linens left - No body - Angelic announcement ## Theological motif Three-day…

from.title: Gall And Vinegar
from.body: # Gall and Vinegar ## The oracle "They gave me poison for food, and for my thirst they gave me sour wine to drink" (Psalm 69:21). ## Date / context Davidic lament psalm, frequently cited in passion narratives. ## Fulfillment Jesus is offered wine mixed with gall and, later, sour wine on a sponge; John notes Jesus says "I thirst" so that Scripture might be fulfilled (John 19:28). ## Connects to `[[…

to.title: Lots Cast For Garments
to.body: # Lots Cast for Garments ## The oracle "They divide my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast lots" (Psalm 22:18). ## Date / context Davidic lament psalm, recited as messianic. ## Fulfillment The soldiers at the cross divide Jesus' clothes and cast lots for the seamless tunic, with John citing the psalm explicitly (John 19:24). ## Connects to `[[../Places/Calvary]]`, `[[../Titles/Messi…

from.title: Feeding Of Four Thousand
from.body: # Feeding of the Four Thousand ## What happens On Gentile territory, Jesus feeds four thousand from seven loaves and a few fish, with seven baskets left over. ## Typological significance A parallel feeding for Gentile crowds — seven baskets evoke the seven nations of Canaan and the Gentile mission. Bread enough for the world, not only Israel. ## Connects to `[[../Numbers]]` (Seven) ## Embedding hi…

to.title: Manna From Heaven
to.body: # Manna From Heaven ## What happens For forty years in the wilderness, Israel gathers daily bread that appears with the dew, sufficient for each day but spoiling if hoarded. ## Typological significance Daily provision that trains dependence — Jesus identifies Himself as the true bread of which manna was the shadow (John 6). The Lord's Prayer's 'daily bread' echoes this rhythm. ## Connects to `[[..…

from.title: Isaiah 9 Child Is Born
from.body: # Isaiah 9 / 'For Unto Us a Child Is Born' → Eternal King ## OT Story — Isaiah 9:6-7 'A child is born, a son is given; the government shall be upon his shoulder. His name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.' ## NT Fulfillment - Luke 1:32-33: He will be great and called Son of the Most High; his kingdom no end. - Luke 2:11: Born this day a Savior, C…

to.title: Zechariah 9 King On Donkey
to.body: # King on a Donkey → Triumphal Entry ## OT Story — Zechariah 9:9-10 'Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion! Behold, your king is coming to you; righteous and having salvation, humble and mounted on a donkey.' ## NT Fulfillment - Matthew 21:4-5: This took place to fulfill what was spoken. - John 12:14-15: Jesus found a young donkey. - Genesis 49:11: Binding his foal to the vine. ## Shared structure -…

from.body: # Daniel 7 — Son of Man Coming ## The oracle "One like a son of man" comes with the clouds of heaven, is presented before the Ancient of Days, and is given dominion, glory, and a kingdom that shall not pass away. ## Date / context Daniel 7, c. 553 BC. ## Fulfillment Jesus identifies himself as this figure under oath before the Sanhedrin (Mark 14:62); Revelation 1:7 frames the second coming with th…
